rationale.R1.1: The positioning task requires reconstructing and validating the paper's contribution claim — that CrowdCausal occupies a unique point in the design space — which depends on correctly articulating the gap the paper fills across three prior-work threads.
rationale.R1.2: The 'first to…' and 'no prior work occupies this point' claims rest on a logical conjunction of three separate exclusions (no quasi-experimental method targets LLM-as-treatment, descriptive studies lack causal identification, pre-LLM QC work predates the contamination problem); internal consistency across these exclusions must be verified.
rationale.R1.4: This is the load-bearing dimension: the delegation is explicitly 'position this against prior work,' which requires assessing whether the three cited literature threads genuinely leave the claimed gap open — i.e., whether the novelty delta is accurate, overstated, or understated relative to nearest prior designs.
rationale.R1.7: Positioning accuracy depends on whether the three bodies of prior work are correctly characterized and whether relevant citations are missing, misattributed, or misrepresented; a gap claim is only as strong as the coverage of the literature it rests on.
